# Beudox HR - Technical Context for AI Assistants

## Architecture Overview

Beudox HR is a modern React-based HR management system built with TypeScript and Vite. It follows a component-driven architecture with clear separation of concerns between UI components, business logic, and data management.

### Technology Stack
- **Frontend Framework**: React 18 with TypeScript
- **Build System**: Vite with SWC for fast compilation
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS with CSS variables for theming
- **UI Components**: shadcn/ui (Radix UI primitives)
- **State Management**: React Query for server state, local component state
- **Routing**: React Router v6 with protected routes
- **Forms**: React Hook Form with Zod validation
- **Backend**: Supabase (PostgreSQL + Auth + Real-time)
- **Charts**: Recharts for data visualization
- **Rich Text**: Tiptap editor for HR policies

## Key Architectural Patterns

### Component Organization
- **Atomic Design**: Components organized by complexity (ui/, layout/, feature-specific/)
- **Composition over Inheritance**: Extensive use of React composition patterns
- **Custom Hooks**: Business logic extracted into reusable hooks
- **TypeScript**: Strict typing throughout with discriminated unions for state management

### Data Flow
- **React Query**: Centralized data fetching with caching and optimistic updates
- **Supabase Client**: Direct database access with Row Level Security
- **Real-time Subscriptions**: Live updates for collaborative features
- **Form Validation**: Zod schemas with React Hook Form integration

### Authentication & Authorization
- **Supabase Auth**: JWT-based authentication with refresh tokens
- **Role-Based Access**: Permission checks at route and component levels
- **Protected Routes**: Automatic redirects based on authentication state
- **Password Recovery**: Secure invite and recovery flows

## Database Schema Highlights

### Core Entities
- **employees**: User profiles with role assignments
- **companies**: Multi-tenant organization data
- **evaluations**: Bi-annual performance reviews
- **daily_evaluations**: Daily feedback system
- **leave_requests**: Leave management with approval workflows
- **payroll_records**: Salary processing with OT calculations
- **projects**: Project management with resource allocation
- **invoices**: Client billing with PDF generation
- **monthly_expenses**: Financial tracking

### Key Relationships
- Employees belong to companies with role-based permissions
- Evaluations link employees to evaluators with scoring systems
- Leave requests have approval chains based on organizational hierarchy
- Payroll integrates with attendance and overtime tracking

## Business Logic Patterns

### Evaluation System
- **Bi-annual Reviews**: Structured evaluation forms with customizable parameters
- **Daily Feedback**: Lightweight peer-to-peer evaluation system
- **Visibility Rules**: Role-based access to evaluation data
- **Timeline Views**: Chronological display of evaluation history

### Financial Calculations
- **Payroll Processing**: Complex calculations including base salary, OT, bonuses, deductions
- **Currency Formatting**: PKR-specific formatting with locale support
- **Trend Analysis**: 6-month rolling financial summaries
- **Expense Tracking**: Monthly expense aggregation with categorization

### Leave Management
- **Balance Calculations**: Accrual-based leave balances with carry-over rules
- **Approval Workflows**: Hierarchical approval processes
- **Calendar Integration**: Working day calculations excluding holidays
- **Policy Enforcement**: Configurable leave types and rules

## Development Workflow

### Code Quality
- **ESLint**: Strict linting with React and TypeScript rules
- **TypeScript**: Strict mode with no implicit any
- **Testing**: Unit tests with Vitest, E2E with Playwright
- **Code Formatting**: Consistent formatting with Prettier

### Build Process
- **Vite**: Fast development server with HMR
- **SWC**: High-performance TypeScript compilation
- **Asset Optimization**: Automatic code splitting and tree shaking
- **Environment Handling**: Environment-specific builds

## Key Files and Their Purposes

### Entry Points
- `src/main.tsx`: Application bootstrap with React 18 createRoot
- `src/App.tsx`: Main routing configuration with protected routes
- `src/pages/Index.tsx`: Legacy entry point (may be deprecated)

### Core Components
- `src/components/layout/AppLayout.tsx`: Main application shell with sidebar navigation
- `src/components/layout/AppSidebar.tsx`: Navigation sidebar with role-based menu items
- `src/components/ui/`: Complete shadcn/ui component library

### Business Logic
- `src/hooks/useAuth.ts`: Authentication state management
- `src/lib/role-access.ts`: Permission checking utilities
- `src/lib/leave-utils.ts`: Leave calculation functions
- `src/lib/format-date.ts`: Date formatting utilities

### Data Layer
- `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts`: Supabase client configuration
- `src/integrations/supabase/types.ts`: Auto-generated TypeScript types from database schema

### Configuration
- `vite.config.ts`: Build configuration with React plugin
- `tailwind.config.ts`: Tailwind CSS configuration with custom theme
- `tsconfig.json`: TypeScript compiler options

## Common Patterns and Conventions

### Component Props
- Use interfaces for complex prop types
- Default props with sensible fallbacks
- Discriminated unions for variant props

### Data Fetching
- React Query hooks for all API calls
- Consistent error handling with toast notifications
- Optimistic updates for better UX

### Styling
- Tailwind utility classes with CSS variables
- Consistent spacing using Tailwind's spacing scale
- Dark mode support with CSS custom properties

### State Management
- Local state for UI concerns
- React Query for server state
- Context for global app state (auth, theme)

## Performance Considerations

- **Code Splitting**: Route-based code splitting with React.lazy
- **Image Optimization**: Lazy loading and responsive images
- **Bundle Analysis**: Tree shaking and dead code elimination
- **Caching**: Aggressive caching with React Query
- **Virtualization**: Not yet implemented but planned for large lists

## Security Measures

- **Row Level Security**: Database-level access control in Supabase
- **Input Validation**: Zod schemas for all user inputs
- **XSS Prevention**: React's automatic escaping
- **CSRF Protection**: Supabase handles token-based auth
- **Secure Headers**: Configured in deployment

## Deployment and Infrastructure

- **Hosting**: Static hosting (Vercel, Netlify) recommended
- **Database**: Supabase managed PostgreSQL
- **CDN**: Automatic asset optimization
- **Environment Variables**: Secure key management
- **Monitoring**: Error tracking and performance monitoring

This technical context should help AI assistants understand the codebase structure, patterns, and conventions used in Beudox HR development.
